summary: Matched the small sysdolphin baselib DevCom accessor HSD_DevComIsBusy by adding a new C implementation file for devcom, defining the backing devComStatus array, declaring the function in devcom.h, and renaming the existing assembly/BSS symbol HSD_DevCom_804C6320 to devComStatus so remaining assembly users continue to reference the same storage.

key_files.role: New C source containing devComStatus[4] and the matched HSD_DevComIsBusy implementation.
Added the public prototype s32 HSD_DevComIsBusy(s32 idx).
Updated remaining assembly references and the BSS label from HSD_DevCom_804C6320 to devComStatus.

what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.c as a new source file.
Defined the BSS/global array as bool devComStatus[4].
Implemented HSD_DevComIsBusy(s32 idx) as a direct indexed return of devComStatus[idx].
Renamed the assembly symbol HSD_DevCom_804C6320 to devComStatus in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.s.
Updated existing assembly references in HSD_DevComIsBusy, HSD_DevComRequest, and HSD_DevComCancelEx to use devComStatus.
smart_moves: The PR did not just add the C function; it also gave the backing BSS object a semantic name, making both the new C and still-undecompiled assembly agree on devComStatus.
Keeping devComStatus visible to assembly let later DevCom functions remain in asm while one small accessor was safely migrated to C.

The matched function was expressed in the simplest possible C form: return devComStatus[idx];, matching the observed PowerPC sequence of scaling the index, loading from the status array, and returning.
decomp_lessons: For small accessors, first identify and name the backing global; a one-line function often becomes obvious once the BSS symbol has a meaningful name.
When only part of a module is decompiled, update assembly labels to the new semantic symbol names so mixed C/asm builds remain consistent.
A BSS .skip size can guide the C declaration: the old symbol reserved 0x10 bytes, corresponding here to a four-entry status array as represented in the matched C.
Add header prototypes at the same time as matching exported or externally used functions so future C migrations can call them without ad hoc extern declarations.

naming_conventions.lesson: Replace address-derived temporary labels with lower-camel semantic data names when the purpose of the global is understood.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_DevComIsBusy
naming_conventions.lesson: Retained the exported HSD_DevCom function naming style for the public API while using a lowercase descriptive name for the internal/global status array.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched HSD_DevComIsBusy as an indexed load from a four-element status array.
The assembly pattern lis/addi base + slwi idx,2 + add + lwz maps directly to devComStatus[idx].
Preserved remaining assembly functions by changing only symbol references, not control flow or instructions.
Used the existing .skip 0x10 BSS allocation to align the C global with the assembly storage footprint.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile HSD_DevComRequest or HSD_DevComCancelEx using devComStatus.
Search for additional address-derived HSD_DevCom_804C63xx symbols that can be semantically named.
Check how bool is typedefed in this codebase when matching BSS arrays loaded with lwz.
